Portfolio

Key Projects & Network Topologies

Campus Network Design

Hierarchical Campus Network Design

Designed a 3-tier hierarchical campus network in Cisco Packet Tracer with a Core router, dual Distribution switches (DS1/DS2), and three Access-layer switches, each serving multiple VLAN-segmented end devices. Connected the core to a simulated ISP edge using loopback interfaces for external reachability testing.

Cisco Packet Tracer Core / Distribution / Access VLAN Segmentation Loopback Interfaces
Download Packet Tracer File (.pkt)

Objectives

Model a standard 3-tier enterprise campus network and validate hierarchical traffic flow from Access through Distribution to the Core, with external reachability to a simulated ISP edge.

Key Features

  • Core router with dual Distribution switches (DS1/DS2) for redundancy
  • Three Access-layer switches serving VLAN-segmented end devices
  • Loopback interfaces on the core for simulated ISP edge testing

Technologies Used

Cisco Packet Tracer Core / Distribution / Access VLAN Segmentation Loopback Interfaces

Documentation

Full Packet Tracer project file available above for download and review (.pkt).

Lessons Learned

Practical grounding in Cisco's hierarchical design model and how VLAN segmentation and redundant distribution switches keep a campus network organized and resilient.

Multi-Area OSPF Design

Enterprise Multi-Area OSPF with RIP Redistribution

Built a multi-area OSPF backbone in GNS3 spanning Area 0, Area 20, Area 40, and Area 80, linked through a central ISP edge router to two external RIPv2 autonomous systems. Implemented route redistribution between the OSPF and RIP domains and validated reachability using loopback interfaces across every area.

GNS3 OSPF Multi-Area RIPv2 Route Redistribution
Download Lab Files (.rar)

Objectives

Design a resilient multi-area OSPF backbone and validate route exchange with two external RIPv2 autonomous systems via redistribution.

Key Features

  • OSPF backbone spanning Area 0, Area 20, Area 40, and Area 80
  • Central ISP edge router linking to two external RIPv2 domains
  • Route redistribution between OSPF and RIP configured and validated
  • Loopback interfaces used to verify reachability across every area

Technologies Used

GNS3 OSPF Multi-Area RIPv2 Route Redistribution

Documentation

Full lab source file available above for download and review (.rar).

Lessons Learned

Hands-on experience with multi-area OSPF design and the practical challenges of redistributing routes cleanly between two different routing protocols.

Routing Integration

Multi-Area OSPF & EIGRP Integration Network

Designed a hybrid enterprise topology in GNS3 combining an OSPF Multi-Area core (Area 0, Area 20, Area 30) with a separate EIGRP Autonomous System. Configured route redistribution between the two routing domains and set up loopback interfaces on edge routers for end-to-end connectivity testing.

GNS3 OSPF Multi-Area EIGRP Route Redistribution

Objectives

Combine two different routing domains into a single, cleanly redistributed enterprise topology and validate end-to-end reachability across both.

Key Features

  • OSPF Multi-Area core spanning Area 0, Area 20, and Area 30
  • Separate EIGRP Autonomous System integrated via redistribution
  • Loopback interfaces on edge routers for connectivity testing

Technologies Used

GNS3 OSPF Multi-Area EIGRP Route Redistribution

Lessons Learned

Practical experience translating route redistribution theory into a working multi-protocol topology, and troubleshooting reachability across routing domain boundaries.

BGP Routing

Enterprise BGP Routing Lab

Designed and implemented a multi-AS BGP enterprise topology, configuring eBGP and iBGP sessions while validating route propagation, path selection, and inter-AS connectivity.

GNS3 eBGP iBGP Multi-AS Topology
Download Lab Files (.rar)

Objectives

Build a realistic multi-AS BGP topology and validate how routes propagate and are selected between autonomous systems.

Key Features

  • Multiple Autonomous Systems interconnected via eBGP
  • iBGP sessions established within each AS for internal route sharing
  • Route propagation and best-path selection validated end-to-end

Technologies Used

GNS3 eBGP iBGP Multi-AS Topology

Documentation

Full lab source file available above for download and review (.rar).

Lessons Learned

Practical experience configuring and troubleshooting BGP peering, path selection, and inter-AS connectivity in an enterprise-style topology.

VPN Connectivity

Dynamic Multipoint VPN (DMVPN) Lab

Built a Dynamic Multipoint VPN (DMVPN) topology connecting multiple branch offices through a central hub, enabling secure communication and scalable site-to-site connectivity.

GNS3 DMVPN Hub-and-Spoke Site-to-Site VPN
Download Lab Files (.rar)

Objectives

Connect multiple branch offices to a central hub over a scalable, secure VPN overlay without requiring a full mesh of static tunnels.

Key Features

  • Central hub router with multiple spoke branch connections
  • Dynamic tunnel establishment between spokes as needed
  • Secure site-to-site communication validated across the topology

Technologies Used

GNS3 DMVPN Hub-and-Spoke Site-to-Site VPN

Documentation

Full lab source file available above for download and review (.rar).

Lessons Learned

Hands-on understanding of scalable VPN overlay design and how DMVPN simplifies branch office connectivity compared to static tunnels.

WAN Redundancy

ISP Path Control & Redundancy Lab

Implemented ISP path control using dual WAN connections to influence traffic forwarding and improve redundancy through route preference and failover techniques.

GNS3 Dual WAN Path Control Failover
Download Lab Files (.rar)

Objectives

Use dual ISP connections to influence outbound and inbound traffic paths and provide automatic failover if one link fails.

Key Features

  • Dual WAN links configured toward two separate ISP edges
  • Route preference techniques used to steer traffic forwarding
  • Failover behavior tested to confirm redundancy on link failure

Technologies Used

GNS3 Dual WAN Path Control Failover

Documentation

Full lab source file available above for download and review (.rar).

Lessons Learned

Practical understanding of influencing route preference and building WAN redundancy so traffic keeps flowing when a link goes down.

Windows Server

Enterprise Active Directory Infrastructure Lab

Designed and deployed a complete enterprise Windows Server 2025 lab using VMware Workstation. Configured Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS), DNS, Organizational Units (OUs), users and groups, Group Policy Objects (GPOs), domain-joined Windows clients, and secure file sharing with permission-based access to simulate a real-world corporate IT infrastructure.

Windows Server 2025 Active Directory DNS Group Policy (GPO) VMware Workstation Windows 10 File Server

Objectives

Build a realistic corporate IT infrastructure in a virtualized lab, covering identity management, policy enforcement, and secure file access.

Key Features

  • Windows Server 2025 deployed with Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS) and DNS
  • Organizational Units (OUs), users, and groups structured for a corporate hierarchy
  • Group Policy Objects (GPOs) applied to domain-joined Windows clients
  • Secure file sharing configured with permission-based access control

Technologies Used

Windows Server 2025 Active Directory DNS Group Policy (GPO) VMware Workstation Windows 10 File Server

Lessons Learned

Practical, end-to-end experience standing up a domain environment: identity, policy, and file access working together the way they would in a real corporate network.
